thesis: ONGC trades cheap on valuation while analyst revisions are strongly positive and price momentum has turned up - the cleanest value-plus-revision agreement in the set, in a name I already hold.
falsification: Crude/realisation collapse or a downgrade cycle turning revisions negative, or the stock lagging the energy basket over the next quarter.

thesis: Chennai Petro is among the cheapest names in the universe with strongly rising revisions; a mean-reversion plus revision bet on refining margins.
falsification: Refining margins compress and revisions roll over, or weak price momentum persists with no re-rating over 3-6 months.

thesis: Grasim pairs strong rising analyst revisions with a fairly valued, diversified franchise (cement + new businesses); a quality-revision core hold.
falsification: Revisions fade or cement pricing/capex cycle disappoints, eroding the earnings-upgrade thesis.

thesis: LIC shows among the strongest rising revisions in the financials cohort at an undemanding valuation - a re-rating-on-upgrades bet in a defensive large insurer.
falsification: Revisions reverse, VNB/margin disappointment, or the stock underperforms private insurers over the next two quarters.

thesis: Muthoot Finance combines strong rising revisions with a high-quality, high-ROE gold-loan book benefiting from elevated gold prices.
falsification: Gold-price reversal or regulatory tightening on gold loans, or revisions turning down.

thesis: NMDC offers low valuation, strong rising revisions and improving price momentum - a cyclical iron-ore play with analyst tailwind.
falsification: Steel/iron-ore demand softens and revisions fade, or momentum rolls over.

thesis: National Aluminium has the strongest momentum among the metals names with rising revisions, riding firm aluminium/alumina prices.
falsification: Aluminium prices fall or the momentum spike unwinds and revisions turn negative.

thesis: Hindalco blends reasonable valuation, positive momentum and rising revisions across aluminium and Novelis - a core metals cyclical hold.
falsification: Novelis spreads compress or base-metal prices drop, with revisions reversing.

thesis: Coal India is cheap with a high dividend yield, rising revisions and firm momentum - a defensive cyclical with cash return.
falsification: Volume/price weakness or an aggressive energy-transition repricing, with revisions fading.

thesis: Gland Pharma shows strong rising revisions with improving momentum as its injectables franchise recovers - a quality-revision pharma bet.
falsification: US generic pricing pressure or facility/regulatory setback turning revisions down.

thesis: Zydus Lifesciences pairs strong rising revisions with a reasonably valued, diversified pharma pipeline - defensive growth.
falsification: Key-product erosion or revisions reversing, or pharma de-rating.

thesis: Coforge has strongly rising revisions reflecting a robust deal pipeline; I take a measured position despite weak near-term momentum.
falsification: Discretionary-IT spend weakens further, deal momentum slows and revisions roll over.

thesis: Vedanta offers strong momentum, decent cheapness and rising revisions - a higher-yield diversified-metals cyclical.
falsification: Commodity downturn or balance-sheet/leverage concerns resurfacing, with momentum and revisions reversing.

thesis: IndusInd is cheap with rising revisions on a recovery view; I hold a modest tactical weight rather than chase given prior governance/asset-quality scars.
falsification: Asset-quality deterioration or deposit flight, or revisions turning negative.

thesis: Solar Industries has strong rising revisions and momentum on a structural defence and mining-explosives order pipeline; sized modestly for rich valuation.
falsification: Order intake slows or rich multiple de-rates as revisions fade.

thesis: Nestle India adds a defensive staple with rising revisions, supporting the fund's low-beta mandate while still carrying an earnings tailwind.
falsification: Rural/volume slowdown persists and revisions reverse, or input-cost spike hits margins.

thesis: Hindustan Zinc pairs rising revisions with positive momentum and a high dividend yield in a low-cost zinc franchise.
falsification: Zinc prices fall or dividend policy disappoints, with revisions rolling over.

thesis: Cholamandalam is a high-quality, high-growth vehicle/MSME financier with rising revisions - a structural NBFC compounder.
falsification: Asset-quality stress in vehicle finance or growth deceleration turning revisions down.

thesis: Bajaj Auto adds an auto franchise with rising revisions, export recovery and strong cash return at a reasonable valuation.
falsification: Domestic two-wheeler demand or export markets weaken and revisions reverse.

thesis: ICICI Bank is the highest-quality private bank with steady compounding; a core financials anchor even though revisions are only neutral.
falsification: NIM compression or asset-quality surprise, or sustained underperformance versus peers.

thesis: HDFC Bank is a cheap, dominant low-beta franchise post-merger normalisation; a defensive core holding for a balanced-advantage book.
falsification: Deposit-cost pressure or growth stall, or revisions deteriorating further.

thesis: SBI is a cheap, dominant PSU bank with healthy credit growth; a value anchor in financials, sized modestly because revisions are soft.
falsification: Credit-cost upcycle or capital-raise dilution, with revisions worsening.

thesis: Axis Bank rounds out the private-bank spine at a reasonable valuation with improving franchise metrics.
falsification: Asset-quality slippage or a return to volatile earnings, with revisions falling.

thesis: Reliance is a core diversified large-cap (energy, retail, telecom) and an index anchor; I keep a neutral weight despite only soft revisions.
falsification: Capex overhang weighs on returns or revisions deteriorate further with no retail/telecom catalysts.

thesis: Bharti Airtel offers structural ARPU-led growth and a defensive cash-flow profile; a low-beta core holding.
falsification: Tariff competition resumes or capex spikes, with revisions turning negative.

thesis: Sun Pharma is the largest, highest-quality Indian pharma with a growing specialty franchise; defensive ballast for the book.
falsification: Specialty-pipeline disappointment or US pricing pressure, with revisions deteriorating.

thesis: ITC is a cheap, high-dividend defensive FMCG/cigarette franchise that supports the fund's low-beta mandate.
falsification: Adverse tax/regulatory action on cigarettes or sustained margin pressure, with revisions falling.

thesis: M&M combines a leading SUV portfolio with a tractor-cycle recovery; a core auto holding at a reasonable valuation.
falsification: SUV demand or tractor cycle weakens and revisions roll over.

thesis: Bajaj Finance is the premier diversified NBFC with durable AUM growth; a structural compounder for the book.
falsification: Growth deceleration or credit-cost normalisation, with revisions worsening.

thesis: Shriram Finance is a cheap, high-yield vehicle financier; I keep a reduced weight given softening revisions.
falsification: Asset-quality stress in CV financing, or further negative revisions.

thesis: Adani Enterprises is the group incubator with rising revisions across airports/new-energy; held at a modest weight given valuation and concentration risk.
falsification: Group-level governance/leverage concerns resurface or project execution slips, revisions reversing.

thesis: Meesho is an e-commerce platform scaling fast; I keep a smaller tactical weight given neutral revisions and unproven profitability.
falsification: Path-to-profitability slips or take-rate compresses, with revisions turning negative.

thesis: Lenskart pairs strong rising revisions with category leadership in eyewear and an omni-channel growth runway.
falsification: Growth/margin disappointment or revisions reversing, or post-listing valuation reset.

thesis: Avenue Supermarts is the most efficient value retailer in India with a long store-expansion runway; defensive consumer compounding.
falsification: Like-for-like growth slows or online competition compresses margins, with revisions falling.

thesis: Bank of Baroda is a cheap PSU bank with healthy return ratios; a value holding within financials, modest given soft revisions.
falsification: Credit-cost upcycle or revisions deteriorating, or PSU-bank de-rating.

thesis: NTPC offers regulated-return stability plus a renewables/thermal capex growth pipeline; a low-beta utility anchor.
falsification: Regulatory return cuts or capex slippage, with revisions turning negative.

thesis: CG Power has rising revisions and rides the power-capex and electrification cycle; a structural industrials holding.
falsification: Order intake slows or rich valuation de-rates as revisions fade.

thesis: Bharat Forge has rising revisions, positive momentum and a defence/aerospace diversification story beyond cyclical forgings.
falsification: CV/export cycle weakens or defence ramp disappoints, with revisions reversing.

thesis: Samvardhana Motherson is a diversified global auto-component consolidator with rising revisions and acquisition-led growth.
falsification: Global auto-production weakness or integration missteps, with revisions falling.

thesis: SBI Life is a high-quality, low-cost life insurer with a strong distribution moat; defensive financials diversification.
falsification: VNB-margin compression or weak premium growth, with revisions deteriorating.

thesis: ICICI Lombard is the leading private general insurer with structural under-penetration tailwinds; a defensive financials holding.
falsification: Combined-ratio deterioration or pricing competition, with revisions turning negative.

thesis: Apollo Hospitals is the premier hospital chain plus a scaling pharmacy/digital platform; a structural healthcare-services compounder.
falsification: Occupancy/ARPOB stalls or digital cash-burn worsens, with revisions falling.

thesis: Varun Beverages pairs rising revisions with a structural soft-drink distribution growth story across India and Africa.
falsification: Volume slowdown or margin pressure from input costs, with revisions reversing.

thesis: Groww is a leading low-cost digital broking and wealth platform with rising revisions and strong customer-acquisition economics.
falsification: Regulatory tightening on broking economics or growth deceleration, with revisions falling.

thesis: Waaree Energies has rising revisions as a leading solar-module manufacturer riding India's renewables capex and domestic-content push.
falsification: Module oversupply/price collapse or order-book softness, with revisions reversing.

thesis: Kotak Mahindra Bank is a high-quality private bank; I keep a reduced core weight given only soft revisions and rich valuation.
falsification: Growth/leadership transition disappoints or revisions worsen.

thesis: Tech Mahindra is a margin-turnaround IT story with neutral-to-positive revisions; I keep a smaller tactical weight pending execution proof.
falsification: Margin-recovery roadmap slips or telecom-vertical weakness, with revisions reversing.

thesis: GAIL is a cheap gas-transmission and marketing utility with volume-growth optionality; a value diversifier, sized small given soft revisions.
falsification: Transmission-tariff cuts or marketing-margin volatility, with revisions deteriorating.

thesis: Infosys remains a high-quality large-cap IT franchise; I keep a reduced weight given weak momentum and only neutral revisions.
falsification: Discretionary-spend recovery fails to materialise and revisions deteriorate.

thesis: AU Small Finance Bank has rising revisions and strong franchise growth; held at a modest weight given valuation and rate-cycle sensitivity.
falsification: Asset-quality stress or NIM pressure from the rate cycle, with revisions reversing.
